title: ANIMA Stage 1 Ground Truth Test: MI Gap > 0 Confirmed

content.purpose: Test whether ANIMA W+I dynamics produce MI gap > 0 under standard protocol, confirming the architecture computes on this task class.

methodology.protocol: Standard harness protocol: 10 seeds, K=[4,6,8,10], n_perm=8, n_trials=6, delta=1.0

conditions.STILL: alive=False — I (mem) stays at zero, no accumulation (control)
conditions.ALIVE: alive=True — I accumulates prediction error via tau=0.3 leaky integrator

methodology.dynamics: Per cell (i,k): neighbor=x[k+1]*x[k-1] (ring topology). W predicts neighbor interaction. Signal couples via gamma. err=actual-w_pred. W learns: w += w_lr*tanh(err). I accumulates (alive only): mem = (1-tau)*mem + tau*tanh(err). State: x_new = tanh(x + w*neighbor + mem*x).

interpretation.ground_truth: PASS — alive_gap > 0 means ANIMA produces sequence-distinguishable final states
interpretation.effect_size: d=0.84 is medium effect. Living Seed achieves d~20+ at canonical params, but ANIMA is uncalibrated prototype with zero parameter tuning. The critical result is gap > 0, not gap magnitude.
interpretation.still_near_zero: STILL gap ≈ 0 (expected) — without I accumulation, no sequence memory, final states are indistinguishable
interpretation.alive_positive: ALIVE gap +0.0274 — I accumulation creates sequence-dependent final states as predicted by W⊕I⊕T theory
interpretation.mechanism_confirmed: Prediction error accumulation in I creates irreversible state history. Different signal orderings produce different I trajectories, producing different final centroids.

implications.architecture_viable: ANIMA computes on this task class. Substrate transition from Living Seed is viable.
implications.calibration_needed: d=0.84 vs Living Seed d~20+ suggests room for parameter optimization in Session 19
implications.stage2_ready: Stage 1 passed → Stage 2 (W-conditioned adaptation) can proceed in Session 19
